tools.name: fetch-article-content
tools.description: Fetches and extracts text + image URLs from a Post's article page.

post.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].

## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You curate articles for a Magazine. The candidate Posts are provided in the <input> as a
  JSON array under "candidates"; each candidate has an "id" plus title, description, author,
  feedName, published, and link. The Magazine's own instructions (provided to you separately)
  describe the Topic — what this magazine should cover.
  
  Select only the candidates that clearly match the Topic — quality over quantity. When a
  candidate's title and description are not enough to judge relevance, you MAY call
  fetchArticleContent with the candidate's id to read the full article text before deciding.
  
  Never select duplicate articles. Two candidates are duplicates if they share a link or guid,
  OR if their titles and content describe the same story (a fuzzy duplicate — e.g. the same
  article syndicated by different feeds). Select only one of each (prefer the most complete or
  most authoritative source) and skip the rest.
  
  When you are done, call completeJob with the structured output:
  { "posts": [{ "id": "<candidate id>" }, ...] }
  listing the ids of the candidates you selected, in the order you want them shown. Only use
  ids that appear in the input — never invent ids. Do not attempt to add Posts yourself; the
  Magazine is updated mechanically from your output.
</blueprint>

Curate articles about space exploration and astronomy — missions, spacecraft, telescopes, and astrophysics discoveries. Exclude unrelated business, finance, or software-tooling news.
<input>{"candidates":[{"id":"01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNT8F","feedName":"Tech & Science Daily","title":"NASA's Artemis II crew completes lunar flyby rehearsal","description":"The four astronauts ran a full simulation of the crewed Moon flyby, practicing re-entry and recovery procedures ahead of next year’s launch.","published":"2026-05-01T00:00:00Z","link":"https://example.com/artemis-ii"},{"id":"01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNT8G","feedName":"Tech & Science Daily","title":"James Webb telescope confirms the most distant galaxy yet seen","description":"Astronomers used JWST spectroscopy to confirm a galaxy as it appeared 290 million years after the Big Bang, a new record for cosmic distance.","published":"2026-05-01T00:00:00Z","link":"https://example.com/jwst-galaxy"},{"id":"01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNT8H","feedName":"Tech & Science Daily","title":"Retail chain beats quarterly earnings estimates","description":"Shares rose 6% after the company reported stronger-than-expected holiday sales and raised its full-year profit guidance.","published":"2026-05-01T00:00:00Z","link":"https://example.com/retail-earnings"},{"id":"01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNT8J","feedName":"Tech & Science Daily","title":"New JavaScript build tool promises 10x faster bundling","description":"The Rust-based bundler aims to replace existing toolchains for large web applications with incremental compilation.","published":"2026-05-01T00:00:00Z","link":"https://example.com/js-bundler"}]}</input>

content.text:
Let me analyze each candidate:

1. "NASA's Artemis II crew completes lunar flyby rehearsal" - Space exploration, missions. Relevant.
2. "James Webb telescope confirms the most distant galaxy yet seen" - Astronomy, telescopes, astrophysics. Relevant.
3. "Retail chain beats quarterly earnings estimates" - Business/finance. Exclude.
4. "New JavaScript build tool promises 10x faster bundling" - Software tooling. Exclude.

Clear-cut selections: candidates 1 and 2. No duplicates. No need to fetch full articles.

The candidates are straightforward to evaluate based on their titles and descriptions:

| # | Candidate | Verdict |
|---|-----------|---------|
| 1 | **Artemis II lunar flyby rehearsal** | ✅ Space mission — clearly on-topic |
| 2 | **JWST confirms most distant galaxy** | ✅ Telescope + astrophysics discovery — clearly on-topic |
| 3 | Retail chain earnings | ❌ Business/finance — excluded per instructions |
| 4 | JavaScript build tool | ❌ Software tooling — excluded per instructions |

No duplicates detected. Selecting the two space/astronomy articles.
